text.title: AWS IAM Security Foundations Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `AWS IAM Security Foundations` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Root account MUST have hardware MFA enabled, no access keys, contact info up to date, billing alerts configured, and last login > 30 days. Root usage MUST emit a CloudWatch alarm.

rationale.source: CIS AWS Benchmark; AWS root account best practices

rule.rationale: Root credentials compromise = entire org compromise; hardening + alarms shrink that surface to near-zero.

rule.statement: CloudTrail MUST be an organisation-wide multi-region trail with log file validation on; KMS-encrypted log delivery to a logging-account S3 bucket with MFA-delete.
rule.rationale: Per-account / single-region trails miss cross-account or non-default-region actions; org-wide multi-region closes that.

rule.statement: GuardDuty MUST be enabled in every account + every region with S3 Protection + Kubernetes Protection + Malware Protection on; findings delegated to a security-tools account.
rule.rationale: Threat detection per-account is fragmented; org-wide GuardDuty surfaces lateral movement.

rule.statement: Account password policy MUST require length ≥ 14, all four character classes, password reuse-prevention ≥ 24, max age ≤ 90 days for IAM users (if any exist).
rule.rationale: Weak password policies are the #1 manual-access failure mode; strict policy + SSO removes 95% of the surface.

rule.statement: Every IAM user MUST have MFA; users without MFA are auto-disabled after 7 days. SSO users MUST have MFA enforced via Identity Center.

rationale.source: AWS IAM Best Practices; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Phished IAM credentials without MFA = full access; MFA enforcement closes that.

rule.statement: Long-lived access keys (if any exist) MUST be rotated ≤ 90 days; keys older than 90d are auto-flagged + scheduled for rotation.
rule.rationale: 90+ day keys accumulate exposure surface; rotation policy keeps blast radius bounded.

rule.statement: AWS Config MUST be enabled in every account + region with the CIS-AWS-Foundations conformance pack deployed; non-compliant resources flagged + remediated within 30 days.
rule.rationale: Drift from baseline is invisible without Config; conformance pack is the cheapest CIS gate.

rule.statement: Security Hub MUST be enabled with org-wide aggregation in a dedicated security-tools account; CIS AWS Foundations + AWS Foundational Security Best Practices standards on.
rule.rationale: Per-account Security Hub is noisy; org-wide aggregation is the single pane of glass auditors need.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
